pattern: Treat each uppercase letter as a base-26 digit where A=1 through Z=26, then accumulate the positional value left to right.
hints: Each character contributes its letter value (A=1, B=2, …, Z=26) multiplied by 26 raised to the power of its distance from the right.
Processing left to right, multiply the running total by 26 before adding the next letter's value — this avoids computing large powers explicitly.
Map each character to its 1-based value with ord(c) - ord('A') + 1, then apply the Horner-scheme accumulation: total = total * 26 + value.

edge_inputs.description: ZZ spans two full cycles of 26
all-Z string to confirm no off-by-one in accumulation
mixed case where adjacent letters create carry-like accumulation

stress.description: random column titles of length 1 to 7
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    length = rng.randint(1, 7)
    title = ''.join(chr(ord('A') + rng.randint(0, 25)) for _ in range(length))
    return [title]
